Title: CeBIT

URL Source: https://en.wikipedia.org/wiki/CeBIT

Published Time: 2001-05-17T18:50:36Z

Markdown Content:
| CEBIT |
| --- |
| [![Image 1](https://upload.wikimedia.org/wikipedia/commons/thumb/a/af/Cebitlogo2018.svg/250px-Cebitlogo2018.svg.png)](https://en.wikipedia.org/wiki/File:Cebitlogo2018.svg) Logo |
| Status | Defunct |
| Genre | Computer Expo |
| Frequency | Annually |
| Venue | [Hanover fairground](https://en.wikipedia.org/wiki/Hanover_fairground "Hanover fairground") |
| Location | [Hanover](https://en.wikipedia.org/wiki/Hanover "Hanover") |
| Country | Germany |
| Inaugurated | 1970 |
| Most recent | 2018 |
| Attendance | 120,000 (2018)[[1]](https://en.wikipedia.org/wiki/CeBIT#cite_note-1) |
| Organized by | [Deutsche Messe AG](https://en.wikipedia.org/wiki/Deutsche_Messe_AG "Deutsche Messe AG") |
| Website | [www.cebit.de](https://www.cebit.de/) |
[![Image 2](https://upload.wikimedia.org/wikipedia/commons/thumb/1/1b/CeBIT_2000_exhibition_hall.jpg/250px-CeBIT_2000_exhibition_hall.jpg)](https://en.wikipedia.org/wiki/File:CeBIT_2000_exhibition_hall.jpg)

A crowded exhibition hall during CeBIT 2000

[![Image 3](https://upload.wikimedia.org/wikipedia/commons/thumb/c/c7/IBM_CeBIT_2010.jpg/250px-IBM_CeBIT_2010.jpg)](https://en.wikipedia.org/wiki/File:IBM_CeBIT_2010.jpg)

[IBM](https://en.wikipedia.org/wiki/IBM "IBM") stand during CeBIT 2010

[![Image 4](https://upload.wikimedia.org/wikipedia/commons/thumb/e/e6/2014-03-14_CeBIT_Global_Conferences%2C_Jimmy_Wales%2C_Founder_Wikipedia%2C_%2826%29_On_stage_showing_the_world_for_Wikipedia_Zero_%28500_millions%29%2C_while_Brent_Goff_is_still_listening.jpg/250px-thumbnail.jpg)](https://en.wikipedia.org/wiki/File:2014-03-14_CeBIT_Global_Conferences,_Jimmy_Wales,_Founder_Wikipedia,_(26)_On_stage_showing_the_world_for_Wikipedia_Zero_(500_millions),_while_Brent_Goff_is_still_listening.jpg)

[Jimmy Wales](https://en.wikipedia.org/wiki/Jimmy_Wales "Jimmy Wales") 2014 on _CeBIT Global Conferences_, [Wikipedia Zero](https://en.wikipedia.org/wiki/Wikipedia_Zero "Wikipedia Zero")

[![Image 5](https://upload.wikimedia.org/wikipedia/commons/thumb/0/07/CEBIT-Besucherzahlen.png/250px-CEBIT-Besucherzahlen.png)](https://en.wikipedia.org/wiki/File:CEBIT-Besucherzahlen.png)

Number of CeBIT visitors 1986–2013

[![Image 6](https://upload.wikimedia.org/wikipedia/commons/thumb/b/b7/Logo_CeBIT.svg/250px-Logo_CeBIT.svg.png)](https://en.wikipedia.org/wiki/File:Logo_CeBIT.svg)

Logo used until 2017

**CeBIT** was a [computer expo](https://en.wikipedia.org/wiki/Trade_show "Trade show") which, at its peak,[_[when?](https://en.wikipedia.org/wiki/Wikipedia:Manual\_of\_Style/Dates\_and\_numbers#Chronological\_items "Wikipedia:Manual of Style/Dates and numbers")_] was the largest[_[citation needed](https://en.wikipedia.org/wiki/Wikipedia:Citation\_needed "Wikipedia:Citation needed")_] and most internationally representative.[_[according to whom?](https://en.wikipedia.org/wiki/Wikipedia:Manual\_of\_Style/Words\_to\_watch#Unsupported\_attributions "Wikipedia:Manual of Style/Words to watch")_] The trade fair was held each year on the [Hanover fairground](https://en.wikipedia.org/wiki/Hanover_fairground "Hanover fairground"), the world's largest fairground, in [Hanover](https://en.wikipedia.org/wiki/Hanover "Hanover"), Germany. In its day, it was considered a barometer of current trends and a measure of the state of the art in [information technology](https://en.wikipedia.org/wiki/Information_technology "Information technology"). It was organized by [Deutsche Messe AG](https://en.wikipedia.org/wiki/Deutsche_Messe_AG "Deutsche Messe AG").[[2]](https://en.wikipedia.org/wiki/CeBIT#cite_note-2)

With an exhibition area of roughly 450,000 m 2 (4.8 million ft 2) and a peak attendance of 850,000 visitors during the [dot-com boom](https://en.wikipedia.org/wiki/Dot-com_boom "Dot-com boom"), it was larger both in area and attendance than its Asian counterpart [COMPUTEX](https://en.wikipedia.org/wiki/COMPUTEX "COMPUTEX") and its no-longer held American equivalent [COMDEX](https://en.wikipedia.org/wiki/COMDEX "COMDEX"). **CeBIT** is a German language [acronym](https://en.wikipedia.org/wiki/Acronym "Acronym") for _**Ce**ntrum für **B**üroautomation, **I**nformationstechnologie und **T**elekommunikation_,[[3]](https://en.wikipedia.org/wiki/CeBIT#cite_note-3) which translates as "Center for Office Automation, Information Technology and Telecommunication".

The final CeBIT took place in 2018.

CeBIT was traditionally the computing part of the [Hanover Fair](https://en.wikipedia.org/wiki/Hanover_Fair "Hanover Fair"), a big industry [trade show](https://en.wikipedia.org/wiki/Trade_show "Trade show") held every year. It was established in 1970, with the opening of the [Hanover fairground](https://en.wikipedia.org/wiki/Hanover_fairground "Hanover fairground")'s new Hall 1, then the largest exhibition hall in the world.[[4]](https://en.wikipedia.org/wiki/CeBIT#cite_note-4) However, in the 1980s the information technology and telecommunications part was straining the resources of the trade fair so much that it was given a separate trade show starting in 1986, which was held four weeks earlier than the main Hanover Fair.

The number of visitors for the new exhibition increased to 830,000 in 2001, but by 2007 the CeBIT expo attendance had shrunk to around 200,000,[[5]](https://en.wikipedia.org/wiki/CeBIT#cite_note-5) then attendance rebounded to 334,000 by 2010.[[6]](https://en.wikipedia.org/wiki/CeBIT#cite_note-6) The 2008 expo was marred by [police raids](https://en.wikipedia.org/wiki/Police_raid "Police raid") of 51 exhibitors for [patent infringement](https://en.wikipedia.org/wiki/Patent_infringement "Patent infringement").[[7]](https://en.wikipedia.org/wiki/CeBIT#cite_note-7) In 2009, the U.S. state of California became official Partner State of Germany's IT and telecommunications industry association, BITKOM, and of CeBIT 2009. focusing on environmentally-friendly technologies.[[8]](https://en.wikipedia.org/wiki/CeBIT#cite_note-8)[Twick.it](https://en.wikipedia.org/wiki/Twick.it "Twick.it") was presented at CeBIT 2010.[[9]](https://en.wikipedia.org/wiki/CeBIT#cite_note-CEBIT-9)

From 2007 till 2013, the fair acted as the World Championship (Grand Final) of [Intel Extreme Masters](https://en.wikipedia.org/wiki/Intel_Extreme_Masters "Intel Extreme Masters"). The championship was reallocated to [Katowice](https://en.wikipedia.org/wiki/Katowice "Katowice"), Poland in 2014.[_[citation needed](https://en.wikipedia.org/wiki/Wikipedia:Citation\_needed "Wikipedia:Citation needed")_]

On 28 November 2018, Deutsche Messe AG announced that due to declining visitor and exhibitioner attendance, CeBIT would be canceled for the foreseeable future.[[10]](https://en.wikipedia.org/wiki/CeBIT#cite_note-10)[[11]](https://en.wikipedia.org/wiki/CeBIT#cite_note-11) This makes CeBIT 2018 the final event.[[12]](https://en.wikipedia.org/wiki/CeBIT#cite_note-12)

## Other CeBIT-branded shows

[[edit](https://en.wikipedia.org/w/index.php?title=CeBIT&action=edit&section=2 "Edit section: Other CeBIT-branded shows")]

As CeBIT continued to grow quickly and was becoming too big on its own, it was decided to concentrate on the professional market, while the home and entertainment market was given a separate show, **CeBIT Home**, during summer, planned to be biennial. However, after being held twice (in 1996 and 1998), the 2000 CeBIT Home (had originally been scheduled to be held in [Leipzig](https://en.wikipedia.org/wiki/Leipzig "Leipzig") due to the [Expo 2000](https://en.wikipedia.org/wiki/Expo_2000 "Expo 2000") being held in Hanover) was cancelled and the project was abandoned.

Since 1999 the CeBIT sponsor [Deutsche Messe AG](https://en.wikipedia.org/wiki/Deutsche_Messe_AG "Deutsche Messe AG") ("German Trade Show, Inc.") has organized trade shows outside of Germany bearing the CeBIT name:

*   [Internet +, powered by CeBIT](http://www.internetplus-expo.com/En/) (formerly CeBIT Asia), in [Shanghai](https://en.wikipedia.org/wiki/Shanghai "Shanghai"), China
*   [CeBIT Australia](https://www.cebit.com.au/)[Archived](https://web.archive.org/web/20190710190824/https://www.cebit.com.au/) 2019-07-10 at the [Wayback Machine](https://en.wikipedia.org/wiki/Wayback_Machine "Wayback Machine"), in [Sydney](https://en.wikipedia.org/wiki/Sydney "Sydney")
*   CeBIT Eurasia Bilişim, in [Istanbul](https://en.wikipedia.org/wiki/Istanbul "Istanbul"), Turkey
*   CeBIT America/USA in New York City, United States.[[13]](https://en.wikipedia.org/wiki/CeBIT#cite_note-13) It was held in 2003 and 2004, but subsequently cancelled in 2005.[[14]](https://en.wikipedia.org/wiki/CeBIT#cite_note-14)
*   CeBIT India, in [Bangalore](https://en.wikipedia.org/wiki/Bangalore "Bangalore"), [India](https://en.wikipedia.org/wiki/India "India")
*   BITS Mexico, powered by CeBIT, in [México City](https://en.wikipedia.org/wiki/Mexico_City "Mexico City").
*   [CeBIT ASEAN Thailand](https://cebitasean.com/), in [Bangkok](https://en.wikipedia.org/wiki/Bangkok "Bangkok"), [Thailand](https://en.wikipedia.org/wiki/Thailand "Thailand").

## CeBIT Global Conferences

[[edit](https://en.wikipedia.org/w/index.php?title=CeBIT&action=edit&section=3 "Edit section: CeBIT Global Conferences")]

Running over a five-day period in [Hanover](https://en.wikipedia.org/wiki/Hanover "Hanover"), [Germany](https://en.wikipedia.org/wiki/Germany "Germany"), the CeBIT Global Conferences (CGC) are staged congruently with the CeBIT exhibition. The conferences are dedicated to providing a 360° overview of the digital industry's four core markets: IT, Telecommunications, Digital Media and Consumer Electronics. Noted industry figures and researchers from across the globe are invited to speak on the latest relevant trends and innovations as well as their impact on society and the working world. The conference is divided up into [keynote](https://en.wikipedia.org/wiki/Keynote "Keynote") speeches, talks and panel discussions. The CGC conferences are produced by Deutsche Messe AG, with the German BITKOM association acting as the CGC patron since 2009. In 2014, the CGC were staged with 140 speakers on three stages with a program of 70 conference hours and 3000 participants. Target groups of the conference are [CXOs](https://en.wikipedia.org/wiki/Chief_Experience_Officer "Chief Experience Officer"), [managers](https://en.wikipedia.org/wiki/Management "Management"), experts, visionaries and out-of-the-box thinkers, Conference languages are English and German.[_[citation needed](https://en.wikipedia.org/wiki/Wikipedia:Citation\_needed "Wikipedia:Citation needed")_]

Recent conferences have featured the following keynote themes:

*   2008: "Improving Life in the Global Village". This installment of CGC attracted 1900 visitors and 43 speakers attending the keynotes and discussion sessions.
*   2009: "How Will We Be Working, Living and Communicating in the Coming Years?" This CGC drew 3,133 visitors from 88 nations, with some 2,200 guests following the conference via live streaming.
*   2010: "The Challenges of a Changing World – ICT for Better Lives and Better Business", attracting some 4,000 guests from more than 100 nations. Just under 4,000 guests also visited the conference via live streaming.[_[citation needed](https://en.wikipedia.org/wiki/Wikipedia:Citation\_needed "Wikipedia:Citation needed")_]

The motto of the CeBIT Global Conferences for 2011 was "**The Power of Creativity and Innovation**".

*   [Steve Wozniak](https://en.wikipedia.org/wiki/Steve_Wozniak "Steve Wozniak"), co-founder [Apple Computer](https://en.wikipedia.org/wiki/Apple_Computer "Apple Computer")
*   [Jimmy Wales](https://en.wikipedia.org/wiki/Jimmy_Wales "Jimmy Wales"), founder [Wikipedia](https://en.wikipedia.org/wiki/Wikipedia "Wikipedia")
*   [Eugene Kaspersky](https://en.wikipedia.org/wiki/Eugene_Kaspersky "Eugene Kaspersky"), CEO and Chairman, [Kaspersky Lab](https://en.wikipedia.org/wiki/Kaspersky_Lab "Kaspersky Lab")
*   [Mark Shuttleworth](https://en.wikipedia.org/wiki/Mark_Shuttleworth "Mark Shuttleworth"), founder, [Canonical](https://en.wikipedia.org/wiki/Canonical_Ltd. "Canonical Ltd.")
*   [Neelie Kroes](https://en.wikipedia.org/wiki/Neelie_Kroes "Neelie Kroes"), Vice President, [European Commission](https://en.wikipedia.org/wiki/European_Commission "European Commission")
*   Sir Nigel Shadbolt, Chairman and co-founder, [Open Data Institute](https://en.wikipedia.org/wiki/Open_Data_Institute "Open Data Institute") (ODI)
*   Dean Douglas, CEO [Unify](https://en.wikipedia.org/wiki/Unify_Software_and_Solutions_GmbH_%26_Co._KG. "Unify Software and Solutions GmbH & Co. KG.")
*   Cristina Riesen, General Manager Europe, [Evernote](https://en.wikipedia.org/wiki/Evernote "Evernote")
*   [Mikko Hyppönen](https://en.wikipedia.org/wiki/Mikko_Hypp%C3%B6nen "Mikko Hyppönen"), Chief Research Officer, [F-Secure](https://en.wikipedia.org/wiki/F-Secure "F-Secure")

Over the past years, speakers at the CeBIT Global Conferences have included Gov. [Arnold Schwarzenegger](https://en.wikipedia.org/wiki/Arnold_Schwarzenegger "Arnold Schwarzenegger"), Governor of California; [Kevin Turner](https://en.wikipedia.org/wiki/Brian_Kevin_Turner "Brian Kevin Turner"), COO, Microsoft, [Craig Barrett](https://en.wikipedia.org/wiki/Craig_Barrett_(businessman) "Craig Barrett (businessman)"), Chairman of the Board, Intel, Jon Iwata SVP Marketing & Communications, IBM, [Reid Hoffman](https://en.wikipedia.org/wiki/Reid_Hoffman "Reid Hoffman"), Chairman and CEO, LinkedIn; Mukund Krishna, CEO Suyati Inc; Scott Durchslag, COO, Skype; [Werner Vogels](https://en.wikipedia.org/wiki/Werner_Vogels "Werner Vogels"), Vice President & CTO, Amazon, [Stewart Butterfield](https://en.wikipedia.org/wiki/Stewart_Butterfield "Stewart Butterfield"), Co-founder of Flickr.com, [Michael T. Jones](https://en.wikipedia.org/wiki/Michael_Jones_(entrepreneur) "Michael Jones (entrepreneur)"), Chief Technology Advocate, Google & Founder of Google Earth; [Mark Kingdon](https://en.wikipedia.org/wiki/Mark_D._Kingdon "Mark D. Kingdon"), CEO, LindenLab.[_[citation needed](https://en.wikipedia.org/wiki/Wikipedia:Citation\_needed "Wikipedia:Citation needed")_]

CeBIT was a platform for recognising achievement by ICT businesses, particularly in Australia.[[15]](https://en.wikipedia.org/wiki/CeBIT#cite_note-15) The awards include the Excellence in Communications Award, the Advanced Retail Technology Award, the Innovative IT Security Award, and the Early Innovators Award. Notable past winners include [Motorola](https://en.wikipedia.org/wiki/Motorola "Motorola"),[[16]](https://en.wikipedia.org/wiki/CeBIT#cite_note-16)[McAfee](https://en.wikipedia.org/wiki/McAfee "McAfee")[[17]](https://en.wikipedia.org/wiki/CeBIT#cite_note-17) and [eWAY](https://en.wikipedia.org/wiki/EWay "EWay").[[18]](https://en.wikipedia.org/wiki/CeBIT#cite_note-18)

*   [List of computer-related awards](https://en.wikipedia.org/wiki/List_of_computer-related_awards "List of computer-related awards")
*   [CES](https://en.wikipedia.org/wiki/Consumer_Electronics_Show "Consumer Electronics Show") (Las Vegas, Nevada, US)
*   [COMPUTEX](https://en.wikipedia.org/wiki/COMPUTEX "COMPUTEX") (Taipei, Taiwan)
*   [SIMO TCI](https://en.wikipedia.org/wiki/SIMO_TCI "SIMO TCI") (Madrid, Spain)
